name: Popover
description: Positionable floating panel with anchor and arrow.

api.name: Popover
api.description: Positionable floating panel with anchor and arrow.

parameters.name: IsOpen
parameters.description: Use Open instead. IsOpen will be removed in a future release.

parameters.name: IsOpenChanged
parameters.description: Use OpenChanged instead. IsOpenChanged will be removed in a future release.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Extra CSS classes for the popover ROOT wrapper div, e.g. w-full to un-shrink-wrap a full-width trigger contained inside. The base classes (relative inline-block outline-none) are always preserved.

events.name: IsOpenChanged
events.description: Use OpenChanged instead. IsOpenChanged will be removed in a future release.

records.name: PopoverContext
records.signature: PopoverContext(bool IsOpen, EventCallback<bool> SetOpen, string WrapperId, string ContentId)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Open Popover</Button>
    </PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent>
        <Stack Gap="4">
            <Stack Gap="2">
                <Heading Level="4" Class="text-base font-medium leading-none">Dimensions</Heading>
                <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Set the dimensions for the layer.</Lumeo.Text>
            </Stack>
            <Grid Columns="3" Gap="4" Class="items-center">
                <Label>Width</Label>
                <Input Class="col-span-2 h-8" Value="100%" />
                <Label>Height</Label>
                <Input Class="col-span-2 h-8" Value="25px" />
            </Grid>
        </Stack>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

examples.title: Alignment
examples.code:
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Start</Button>
    </PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Align="Lumeo.Align.Start">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Aligned to start.</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger>
        <Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">End</Button>
    </PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Align="Lumeo.Align.End">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Aligned to end.</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

examples.title: With Arrow
examples.code:
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button></PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Top" ShowArrow="true">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Popover on top with an arrow pointing at the trigger.</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

examples.title: Placement
examples.code:
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Top</Button></PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Top">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Popover on top</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Bottom</Button></PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Bottom">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Popover on bottom</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Left</Button></PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Left">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Popover on left</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
<Popover>
    <PopoverTrigger><Button Variant="Button.ButtonVariant.Outline">Right</Button></PopoverTrigger>
    <PopoverContent Side="Lumeo.Side.Right">
        <Lumeo.Text Size="sm">Popover on right</Lumeo.Text>
    </PopoverContent>
</Popover>

parameters.name: FocusOnOpen
parameters.description: When true (default), focus moves into the popover surface on open so its @onkeydown handler receives Escape and keyboard users land inside the dialog (the Radix onOpenAutoFocus default). Set to false when the trigger is a typeable control that must KEEP focus while the popover opens (e.g. DatePicker's keyboard-input trigger) — the surface still positions, registers click-outside and saves/restores focus, it just doesn't steal focus from the trigger on open.

parameters.name: ChildContentSlot
parameters.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onclick="slot.OnClick.InvokeAsync" onto their own single element. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

parameters.name: AsChild
parameters.description: When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — yielding ONE DOM node with merged class, composed click/keyboard, and forwarded ARIA/state (the Radix asChild goal). The child must resolve to exactly one cooperating element (a Lumeo <Button>, or a manual ChildContentSlot splat); raw HTML/3rd-party markup receives nothing. Default false preserves the current role=button wrapper exactly. Prefer a <button> child for a click-toggle trigger: a <Button Href> renders an <a>, which natively activates on Enter only (not Space), so Space would not open the popover.

slots.name: ChildContentSlot
slots.description: Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onclick="slot.OnClick.InvokeAsync" onto their own single element. Lumeo <Button> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

mdSummary:
# Popover

Positionable floating panel with anchor and arrow.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Overlay
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/popover

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Popover | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| Popover | Open | `bool` | `false` |  |
| Popover | OpenChanged | `EventCallback<bool>` | `` |  |
| Popover | IsOpen | `bool` | `false` | Use Open instead. IsOpen will be removed in a future release. |
| Popover | IsOpenChanged | `EventCallback<bool>` | `` | Use OpenChanged instead. IsOpenChanged will be removed in a future release. |
| Popover | Class | `string?` | `` | Extra CSS classes for the popover ROOT wrapper div, e.g. w-full to un-shrink-wrap a full-width trigger contained inside. The base classes (relative inline-block outline-none) are always preserved. |
| Popover | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| PopoverContent | Context | `Popover.PopoverContext` | `default!` |  |
| PopoverContent | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| PopoverContent | Align | `Lumeo.Align` | `Lumeo.Align.Center` |  |
| PopoverContent | Side | `Lumeo.Side` | `Lumeo.Side.Bottom` |  |
| PopoverContent | ShowArrow | `bool` | `false` |  |
| PopoverContent | FocusOnOpen | `bool` | `true` | When true (default), focus moves into the popover surface on open so its @onkeydown handler receives Escape and keyboard users land inside the dialog (the Radix onOpenAutoFocus default). Set to false when the trigger is a typeable control that must KEEP focus while the popover opens (e.g. DatePicker's keyboard-input trigger) — the surface still positions, registers click-outside and saves/restores focus, it just doesn't steal focus from the trigger on open. |
| PopoverContent | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| PopoverContent | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| PopoverTrigger | Context | `Popover.PopoverContext` | `default!` |  |
| PopoverTrigger | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| PopoverTrigger | ChildContentSlot | `RenderFragment<TriggerSlot>?` | `` | Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onclick="slot.OnClick.InvokeAsync" onto their own single element. Lumeo \<Button\> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically. |
| PopoverTrigger | AsChild | `bool` | `false` | When true, the trigger renders NO wrapper element and instead cascades a TriggerSlot that a cooperating child folds onto its own single root element — yielding ONE DOM node with merged class, composed click/keyboard, and forwarded ARIA/state (the Radix asChild goal). The child must resolve to exactly one cooperating element (a Lumeo \<Button\>, or a manual ChildContentSlot splat); raw HTML/3rd-party markup receives nothing. Default false preserves the current role=button wrapper exactly. Prefer a \<button\> child for a click-toggle trigger: a \<Button Href\> renders an \<a\>, which natively activates on Enter only (not Space), so Space would not open the popover. |
| PopoverTrigger | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| PopoverTrigger | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Popover) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (PopoverContent) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (PopoverTrigger) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContentSlot` (PopoverTrigger) — Tier-2 escape hatch for AsChild with arbitrary (non-Lumeo) markup: receives the TriggerSlot so the consumer can splat @attributes="slot.Attributes" and wire @onclick="slot.OnClick.InvokeAsync" onto their own single element. Lumeo \<Button\> children need only AsChild — they consume the slot automatically.

## Events
- `OpenChanged` (`EventCallback<bool>`)
- `IsOpenChanged` (`EventCallback<bool>`) — Use OpenChanged instead. IsOpenChanged will be removed in a future release.

## Sub-components
- `PopoverContent`, `PopoverTrigger`

## Cascading values it expects
- `Context` (`Popover.PopoverContext`)

## Services it injects
- `IComponentInteropService` ()
- `IComponentInteropService` (Lumeo.Services)

## Keyboard
- `Escape` — HandleKeyDown handles Escape in PopoverContent.razor

## Example
```razor
<Popover />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-border`, `--color-popover`, `--color-popover-foreground`, `--radius`

## Files
- `UI/Popover/Popover.razor`
- `UI/Popover/PopoverContent.razor`
- `UI/Popover/PopoverTrigger.razor`
